content: Data integrity risks in stock transaction systems: race conditions when two concurrent requests both read stock=1 and both decrement (resulting in stock=-1), no ACID transaction wrapping of stock-in/stock-out operations, allowing negative stock quantities in the database, and orphaned transactions (stock movement recorded but PO not updated or vice versa). Prevention: use database transactions (BEGIN/COMMIT) around all inventory mutations, apply row-level locking (SELECT FOR UPDATE) or optimistic locking with version columns, add database-level CHECK constraints to prevent negative stock, and enforce foreign key integrity between movements and POs.
